question: A 3D mural
answer: This story drives the camera across a 3D model as you scroll. We begin with a wide, straight-on view of the whole mural — it is nearly nine metres across.

question: Into the centre
answer: The camera moves straight in to a detail at the centre. model-viewer interpolates smoothly between the authored positions as the step changes.

question: Scan to the left
answer: Now we pan across the surface to the left — the look-at point shifts, so a flat object can be read across, not just orbited.

question: Orbit around
answer: Here the camera swings around to the left and looks at the mural from a raking angle, so you can read the relief and depth of the mosaic.

question: The other side
answer: And it orbits across to the opposite side — the same surface seen from the right.

question: From above
answer: The camera rises and tips down to look over the top edge of the mural.

question: Around the back
answer: The camera swings right around to look from behind the left shoulder, almost edge-on.

question: Across the seam
answer: It carries on past the back to the matching angle on the other side — taking the short way across, not the long way round the front.

question: Back to the whole
answer: Finally the camera returns to the full, straight-on framing we started from.
